race.name: Noquemanon Ski Marathon
race.tagline: Fifty kilometers and a thousand feet of net descent through Michigan's Upper Peninsula — Lake Superior's snow belt delivers the goods every January.

vitals.aid_stations: Multiple aid stations with warm drinks and food

climate.description: Michigan's Upper Peninsula receives massive lake-effect snowfall from Lake Superior, averaging over 200 inches per season. January temperatures range from -20°C to -5°C. The lake-effect snow machine makes this one of the snowiest regions in the eastern United States.
challenges: Heavy lake-effect snowfall can dump fresh snow on race day
Deep cold in late January with wind chill factor
Trail conditions can vary from packed powder to fresh dump
Iron ore country terrain with rolling, wooded hills

course.primary: Point-to-point from Ishpeming to Marquette with approximately 1,000 feet of net descent. The course rolls through the iron range hills of the Upper Peninsula, mixing hardwood and conifer forest with old mining country. Technical sections through hilly terrain demand confident descending skills.
course.surface: Machine-groomed trails through Upper Peninsula forest and former mining land
course.grooming: Well-groomed by dedicated UP volunteer crews
features: Net descent of approximately 1,000 feet from Ishpeming to Marquette
Through historic iron mining country of the UP
Lake Superior snow belt ensures deep, reliable snowpack
Rolling hardwood and conifer forest terrain
Finish in downtown Marquette overlooking Lake Superior
Part of the Noquemanon Trail Network

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Lake Superior's snow belt provides among the most reliable snow conditions in the eastern US. Strong regional community but limited international profile. The net-descent course and iron country terrain make for a unique racing experience.

history.summary: Founded in 2001, the Noquemanon Ski Marathon takes its name from a Chippewa word meaning 'bear' — fitting for a race through the rugged wilderness of Michigan's Upper Peninsula. The event was created to showcase the exceptional Nordic skiing conditions created by Lake Superior's snow belt and to connect the historic mining towns of Ishpeming and Marquette.
notable_facts: Name derives from Chippewa word 'noquemanon' meaning 'bear'
Michigan's Upper Peninsula receives over 200 inches of snow annually from lake effect
Ishpeming is home to the US National Ski Hall of Fame
The UP was historically iron and copper mining country
Part of the 80+ mile Noquemanon Trail Network
Marquette finish overlooks Lake Superior, the world's largest freshwater lake by surface area
